content:
- Used add-task-as-agent and add-meta-task skill instructions to create new task artifacts under .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness.
- Added 17-task-deep-skeptical-codebase-review-model-gated.md with the exact requested model gate, no-test scope, deep skeptical review criteria, issue fanout rules, and final model reset to normal_high.
- Added 18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md as a true meta-task with required warning line, recurring/fresh-run rules, explicit never-change-passes note, and exploration template.
- Added very short ordered follow-up tasks 19/20/21 that each say to do meta-task 18, chained with blocked_by for sequence over time.
- Did not run tests, did not modify runtime code, and did not revert any unrelated dirty-worktree changes.
- Next: if asked, can create additional short scheduling tasks (22/23/...) or adjust wording/ordering conventions.

- Read prior memory + current task pointer, loaded bug task file, and found marker at TO BE VERIFIED.
- Performed deep skeptical verification with broad parallel code scans; updated task plan with additional order/thread-sensitivity checks and switched marker to NOW EXECUTE.
- Implemented explicit real-test fixture ownership in src/process/worker.rs (`RealProcessFixture`) so StateSubscriber, worker context, sender, and NamespaceGuard live together for full async job lifecycle.
- Moved real promote/demote/start-stop/restart/fencing tests to fixture submit/wait helpers and added explicit subscriber snapshot reads during outcome polling for diagnostics.
- Added regression test `start_job_returns_channel_closed_when_all_subscribers_are_dropped` to prove channel-close semantics when all receivers are gone.
- Hit known stale-target linker artifact error (`missing *.rcgu.o`), recovered with `cargo clean`, then reran targeted tests and full required gates successfully.
- Captured evidence logs under `.ralph/evidence/process-worker-real-job-tests-state-channel-closed-20260302/` and updated bug task file to done/true/passing true.
- Ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`, updated AGENTS.md with a new cross-app learning about avoiding underscore-only lifetime assumptions, and committed everything with hash 154d4f4.
- Next step after quitting: pick up the next task from updated Ralph pointers.

- Read current task inventory from .ralph/current_tasks.md after loading prior progress.
- Evaluated selection rules: pending false tasks exist, no bug had explicit <priority>, and story-rust-system-harness has pending ultra_high tasks.
- Applied mandatory priority + story-chain ordering and selected earliest unblocked ultra_high chain task: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/12-task-ha-loop-integration-tests-real-watchers-and-step-once.md. Wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- User challenged duplicate non-meta task creation and ultra_high priority assignment.
- Compared task 17 vs task 18; determined differences were mostly formatting/context wording and not core requirements.
- Deleted duplicate non-meta task 17 to keep one canonical meta-task as requested.
- Updated meta-task 18 text to explicitly include: trust nothing, assume nothing, and nothing out of scope for code smell/quality review.
- Removed priority tag from meta-task header to avoid adding unrequested priority levels.
- Kept short sequencing tasks 19/20/21 that instruct doing meta-task 18 at different times.

- Updated meta-task 18 per user request to add final-phase test execution after code-smell/findings pass.
- Removed prior no-tests rule from task 18 and added explicit final gates: make check, make test, make lint, make test-bdd.
- Added requirement that any failing final-phase gate must create add-bug task(s) with actionable details.
- Verified updated file contents reflect the new requirement while preserving meta-task invariants.

- Loaded prior memory, read `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and opened task `12-task-ha-loop-integration-tests-real-watchers-and-step-once.md`.
- Performed broad parallel exploration across HA/DCS/process/test-harness/contract files (10+ parallel scans) to ground an execution plan in real code paths.
- Wrote a full detailed execution plan into task 12 with concrete fixture architecture, required HA phase scenarios, run-vs-step parity checks, validation gates, and bookkeeping steps.
- Added `TO BE VERIFIED` at end of task file and intentionally stopped before any execution edits/tests, per requested workflow.
- Next step after restart: read task 12, detect `TO BE VERIFIED`, run deep skeptical plan review, alter at least one plan item, then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Read progress log and inspected repository structure (root, src submodules, tests).
- Listed module directories/files and checked src/lib.rs for module declarations.
- Next: if user wants deeper structure, open specific modules or generate a tree-like map.

- Reviewed core modules (config, state, pginfo, dcs, process, ha, api, debug_api) to assess architecture and dependency flow.
- Noted lint-allow for unwrap/expect/panic in src/test_harness/mod.rs; created bug task to remove allowance and fix usages.
- Next: deliver architectural assessment and dependency layering summary to user, with file references.
